3. Determine which label from the provided options best matches the Target Image.

- Use the examples to infer discriminative visual patterns for each class.
- Do not use external world knowledge, hidden assumptions, or speculative attributes.

- Choose exactly one final label.

raw_response_text:
```xml
<observations>
    <class_44>
        <features>
            <petal_color>deep purple with white inner petal edges</petal_color>
            <center>dark brown to black center with yellowish stamens</center>
            <shape>rounded petals with a slightly ruffled edge</shape>
        </features>
    </class_44>
    <class_45>
        <features>
            <petal_color>bright yellow-orange with reddish-brown accents</petal_color>
            <center>dark brown center</center>
            <shape>petals with a slightly irregular, wavy edge</shape>
        </features>
    </class_45>
    <class_77>
        <features>
            <petal_color>pink to light purple</petal_color>
            <center>bright yellow center with a dense cluster of stamens</center>
            <shape>large, broad, and flat petals with a smooth texture</shape>
        </features>
    </class_77>
</observations>

<target_analysis>
    <features>
        <petal_color>deep purple with white inner edges</petal_color>
        <center>dark brown center with yellowish stamens</center>
        <shape>rounded petals with a slightly ruffled edge</shape>
    </features>
</target_analysis>

<response>44</response>
```
